resolve_rule: epoch_day = u16::try_from(ts / 86_400_000).map_err(|_| WrongRootAccount)?  // checked cast, never `as u16`
notes: Live probe (probe-epochseam.ts) scanned 41 intervals across 5 epoch-days (20632-20636), covering 3 fixtures and 109 stat-validation bundles. Near-midnight coverage included hours 22, 23, 0, 1 for all 5 days. Populated near-midnight slots found on epoch-days 20632 (hours 0, 1) and 20633 (hours 22, 23): in all cases floor(ts/86_400_000) == floor(minTimestamp/86_400_000) — both land on the same epoch-day. The dev feed is sparse (daytime match data only; no game straddling UTC midnight was available), so a true straddle is not directly observable, but this is the expected outcome per spec. The epoch_day source is locked to ts (spike-verified: ts=1782788706633 → epochDay=20634 → PDA BcLwqHJehs8ut8ycRo6NhCGsrtmRnkZbFMm273SdcPGe → confirmed EXISTS on devnet with correct proof). The resolve rule uses u16::try_from (checked conversion) — never a silent `as u16` cast — per design §2.2. Step 2 (discriminating CPI probe) was skipped: no straddle found, so both PDAs do not exist and no discriminating test is meaningful.
